content:
First we index the set  
   M={1,2,4,5,7,8,10,11,…}  
by  
   M_{2k−1}=3k−2,   M_{2k}=3k−1.  

We look for 2n consecutive terms M_s,…,M_{s+2n−1} whose sum is 300.  One checks separately the two cases “s odd” and “s even,” and in each case reduces to a quadratic in n and the starting‐index parameter.  In both cases one finds that the only positive integer solutions are

n=1,2,4,5,10.

Indeed for example  

• n=1:  M_{100}+M_{101}=149+151=300  
• n=2:  M_{49}+M_{50}+M_{51}+M_{52}=73+74+76+77=300  
• n=4,5,10 likewise.  

Hence the possible values of n are

\boxed{1,2,4,5,10}.
